title: Campyloneurum repens
intro: Campyloneurum repens, also known as the creeping polypody or creeping fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions including arthritis and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Campyloneurum repens include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
